cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
cees: noun. plural of cee, the name of the letter C.
cel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Celtic languages.
cere: noun. A fleshy, waxy area at the base of the upper beak in certain birds.
cess: noun. An assessed tax, duty, or levy; billeting.
clerk: noun. One who occupationally provides assistance by working with records, accounts, letters, etc.; an office worker.
creek: noun. A small inlet, often saltwater, leading to the sea or to the main channel of a river, especially a river estuary.
creel: noun. A woven basket, especially a wicker basket.
cress: noun. A plant of various species, chiefly cruciferous.
eel: noun. Any freshwater fish of the order Anguilliformes, which are elongated and resemble snakes.
eke: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ekit.
elk: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Elkei.
else: adjective. Other; in addition to previously mentioned items.
ers: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ersu.
esker: noun. A long, narrow, sinuous ridge created by deposits from a stream running beneath a glacier.
ess: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Central Siberian Yupik.
keel: noun. A large beam along the underside of a ship’s hull from bow to stern.
lee: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lyélé.
leek: noun. A vegetable of variety Allium ampeloprasum, having edible leaves and an onion -like bulb but with a milder flavour than the onion.
leer: verb. To look sideways or obliquely; now especially with sexual desire or malicious intent.
lees: noun. The sediment that settles during fermentation of beverages, consisting of dead yeast and precipitated parts of the fruit.
lek: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Leipon.
lesser: adjective. Of two (or, rarely, more than two) things: the smaller in size (littler), in value, in importance etc.
recess: noun. A depressed, hollow, or indented space; also, a hole or opening.
reck: verb. To take account of (someone or something); to care for; to consider, to heed, to regard.
reckless: adjective. Careless or heedless; headstrong or rash.
recks: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of reck
ree: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rejang Kayan.
reel: noun. A shaky or unsteady gait.
scree: noun. Loose stony debris on a slope.
sec: symbol. Symbol of the trigonometric function secant.
see: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seneca.
seek: verb. To try to find; to look for; to search for.
seeks: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of seek
seels: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of seel
seer: noun. One who foretells the future; a clairvoyant, prophet, soothsayer or diviner.
sees: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of see
sel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Selkup.
ser: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Serrano.
sleek: adjective. Having an even, smooth surface; smooth
sleeks: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of sleek
cesser: noun. This term needs a definition.
crl: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Northern East Cree.
crs: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seychellois Creole.
eek: interjection. Representing a scream or shriek (especially in comic strips and books).
eeks: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of eek
erk: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for South Efate.
ese: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ese Ejja.
lese: verb. Alternative form of leese (“to lose”)
sclere: noun. A skeletal element in sponges.
seckles: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of seckle
skeel: noun. A shallow wooden vessel for holding milk or cream.
skeers: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of skeer
